title: What new income streams have appeared
subtitle: A journal set for taking stock of income that arrived and building evidence of financial growth.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 49 of the MONEY program. The prompts ask you to name every income stream from the current period — including ones you've been minimising.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 49 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a month or quarter to build a proper account of what arrived financially.

content.text: A journal set from Day 49 of the MONEY program. The purpose is to name what arrived financially over a recent period — and to recognise streams that were dismissed or minimised at the time.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Over the last month, what income arrived?
List all of it. Main income, side income, unexpected income, any paid work of any kind. Don't filter by size.
Which of those streams did you barely notice when the money arrived?
Name them. Write what you told yourself about why they didn't count.
What new income streams have appeared in the last six months that weren't present before?
Write each one. Include things you've dismissed as too small or too inconsistent to count.
What one income stream are you most grateful for right now?
Write specifically why. The amount, the source, what it made possible.
What would you need to believe about money for steady, small streams to feel like genuine progress?
Write it as a statement you could carry into next week.
Adapted from Day 49 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
